sourceFrame.primaryClassification: WHO Classification of Tumours: Urinary and Male Genital Tumours, 5th Edition, Volume 8, IARC, 2022
reviewSources: IARC publication page for WHO Classification of Tumours: Urinary and Male Genital Tumours, 5th Edition, Volume 8
Moch et al. European Urology 2022 review of renal, penile, and testicular WHO 2022 updates
Berney et al. Histopathology 2022 introduction to the WHO 5th edition testicular tumour classification
Menon et al. Histopathology 2023 WHO 2022 penile and scrotal cancer update
sourceFrame.curationNote: This manifest is an operational coverage scaffold for resident teaching. It should be reconciled against licensed WHO Blue Book text/WHO Online before publication as a definitive entity checklist.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.entity: Low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion / condyloma / PeIN 1
entities.clinical_context: Papillary or verruciform mucosal lesion, often HPV-related.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Small biopsy or excision; preserve orientation if close to invasive carcinoma.
entities.morphology_anchor: Koilocytic low-grade squamous proliferation without destructive stromal invasion.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p16 is not used as a stand-alone malignancy test; HPV testing is contextual.
entities.reporting_consequence: Do not overcall invasive carcinoma; state absence of invasion when evaluable.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of condyloma acuminatum
visualAids.caption: Condyloma/LSIL showing low-grade HPV-type squamous proliferation.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Flat, erythematous, plaque-like, or papillary lesion; precursor to HPV-associated SCC.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Biopsy diagnosis should trigger careful search for invasion.
entities.morphology_anchor: High-grade squamous atypia with basaloid or warty architecture and intact basement membrane.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Block-type p16 supports transcriptionally active HPV-associated disease.
entities.reporting_consequence: Report as precursor lesion and comment on invasion if tissue permits.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of penile intraepithelial neoplasia
visualAids.caption: Penile intraepithelial neoplasia image for HSIL/PeIN2-3 pattern recognition.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Often arises in chronically inflamed or lichen sclerosus-associated mucosa.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Biopsy may be subtle; correlate with adjacent keratinizing invasive SCC.
entities.morphology_anchor: Basal atypia with premature maturation rather than full-thickness basaloid atypia.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Usually p16 negative or non-block; p53 may be aberrant but is supportive only.
entities.reporting_consequence: High-risk HPV-independent precursor; search carefully for invasive SCC.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of differentiated intraepithelial neoplasia
visualAids.caption: Differentiated intraepithelial neoplasia showing basal atypia with preserved superficial maturation.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of lichen sclerosus
visualAids.caption: This lichen sclerosus image shows dermal sclerosis and chronic inflammatory context; in penile mucosa, that context should prompt careful search for differentiated PeIN and keratinizing SCC.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Invasive SCC with HPV-associated morphology or block-type p16 support.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Biopsy, partial penectomy, or total penectomy; orient to glans, foreskin, urethra, and corpora.
entities.morphology_anchor: Invasive squamous carcinoma in the HPV-associated spectrum without a more specific dominant subtype.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Block-type p16 is the practical daily discriminator when morphology is equivocal.
entities.reporting_consequence: Classify HPV association and document invasion depth, anatomic structures, margins, LVI, and PNI.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of penile squamous cell carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Invasive penile squamous cell carcinoma without a specific dominant subtype.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Aggressive HPV-associated carcinoma; may be deeply invasive at presentation.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Penectomy specimen requires depth and corporal/urethral mapping.
entities.morphology_anchor: Nests or sheets of basaloid cells, often with comedonecrosis or abrupt keratinization.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Block p16 supports HPV association; p40/p63 confirm squamous lineage.
entities.reporting_consequence: High-risk subtype; report grade, depth, LVI/PNI, and margin status precisely.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of basaloid-appearing penile intraepithelial neoplasia
visualAids.caption: This high-grade HPV-associated squamous neoplasia image shows basaloid cytology; correlate with block-pattern p16 and exclude invasive basaloid SCC.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Exophytic HPV-associated carcinoma that can be mistaken for condyloma.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Papillary mass; submit base generously to prove or exclude invasion.
entities.morphology_anchor: Condylomatous papillae with atypia and destructive invasion.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p16 block positivity supports HPV-associated carcinoma.
entities.reporting_consequence: Avoid benign overcall; invasion and depth determine management.

visualAids.alt: Intermediate-power H&E micrograph of penile intraepithelial neoplasia
visualAids.caption: Warty/HPV-associated penile squamous neoplasia reference showing papillary intraepithelial disease.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Mixed HPV-associated morphology with both papillary/warty and basaloid areas.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Sample heterogeneous regions and report meaningful components.
entities.morphology_anchor: Warty exophytic areas admixed with basaloid invasive carcinoma.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Block p16 supports HPV-associated pathogenesis.
entities.reporting_consequence: Component recognition helps risk communication and future classification refinement.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of penile intraepithelial neoplasia
visualAids.caption: Mixed warty-basaloid teaching reference for HPV-associated high-grade squamous neoplasia.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Rare HPV-associated pattern with clear cytoplasm.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Biopsy may prompt metastatic differential; preserve tissue for lineage workup.
entities.morphology_anchor: Invasive squamous carcinoma with ample clear cytoplasm and often necrosis.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p40/p63 and p16 support squamous HPV-associated classification; exclude metastatic clear cell tumors when needed.
entities.reporting_consequence: Do not lose the penile SCC staging workflow while excluding metastasis.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of penile squamous cell carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Penile squamous cell carcinoma with features that should be compared against clear-cell mimics.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Rare HPV-associated SCC pattern with dense inflammatory background.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Biopsy may resemble lymphoma or inflammatory lesion.
entities.morphology_anchor: Poorly differentiated carcinoma cells in dense lymphoplasmacytic/eosinophilic infiltrate.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p40/p63 and p16 support classification; hematolymphoid markers exclude lymphoma.
entities.reporting_consequence: Confirm epithelial lineage and still report invasion/margins.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of penile squamous cell carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Poorly differentiated penile squamous cell carcinoma in an inflamed background.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Keratinizing invasive SCC, often linked to chronic inflammatory dermatoses.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Map glans, foreskin, urethra, corpus spongiosum, corpus cavernosum, margins.
entities.morphology_anchor: Invasive keratinizing squamous carcinoma without HPV-associated features.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p16 non-block pattern supports HPV-independent classification in the right morphology.
entities.reporting_consequence: If HPV status cannot be determined, use SCC NOS; staging parameters remain decisive.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of penile squamous cell carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Keratinizing penile SCC reference for HPV-independent SCC NOS.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Most common HPV-independent invasive penile SCC pattern.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Penectomy sampling should capture deepest invasion and closest margins.
entities.morphology_anchor: Keratinizing invasive SCC; pseudohyperplastic and pseudoglandular/acantholytic patterns sit within the usual-type spectrum.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p40/p63 positive; p16 usually negative or non-block.
entities.reporting_consequence: Depth, anatomic compartment, grade, LVI/PNI, and margins drive treatment.

visualAids.alt: Low-power H&E micrograph of penile squamous cell carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Usual-type penile SCC reference emphasizing invasive architecture.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Slow-growing exophytic or burrowing HPV-independent carcinoma.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Submit broad pushing base and any conventional invasive component.
entities.morphology_anchor: Well-differentiated verruciform carcinoma with broad pushing invasion; cuniculatum shows endophytic labyrinthine channels.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p16 usually non-block; diagnosis is morphology-led.
entities.reporting_consequence: Identify mixed conventional SCC component and report percentages when present.

visualAids.alt: Composite H&E micrograph of penile verrucous carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Penile verrucous carcinoma composite image showing exophytic growth and broad pushing front.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Exophytic papillary HPV-independent SCC.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Base sampling is mandatory to distinguish papillary growth from destructive invasion.
entities.morphology_anchor: Papillae lined by well- to moderately differentiated malignant squamous cells.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p16 generally non-block; morphology and invasion pattern dominate.
entities.reporting_consequence: State invasion depth and exclude/identify mixed usual SCC.

visualAids.alt: Low-power H&E micrograph of penile verrucous carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Papillary/verruciform penile squamous cell carcinoma; assess the base for invasion pattern.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Aggressive high-risk penile carcinoma with spindle-cell morphology.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Large ulcerated or deeply invasive mass; sample transitions to conventional carcinoma.
entities.morphology_anchor: Malignant spindle or pleomorphic cells with evidence of squamous differentiation.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Cytokeratin/p40 may be focal; broad panel excludes melanoma and sarcoma.
entities.reporting_consequence: Flag aggressive biology and document all high-risk staging features.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of sarcomatoid squamous cell carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Sarcomatoid squamous cell carcinoma with malignant spindle-cell morphology.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Rare carcinoma with squamous and glandular/mucinous differentiation.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Sample for mixed components and exclude urethral or metastatic origin.
entities.morphology_anchor: Invasive carcinoma with squamous and glandular or mucin-producing elements.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Squamous markers plus mucin/glandular markers; use anatomy to exclude secondary tumors.
entities.reporting_consequence: Report as distinct entity and document component extent when mixed.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of adenosquamous carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Adenosquamous carcinoma showing squamous and glandular differentiation.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Tumor with two or more meaningful SCC subtypes/patterns.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Submit heterogeneous gross regions and deepest invasive front.
entities.morphology_anchor: Multiple invasive SCC patterns, such as verrucous/cuniculatum with usual-type invasive SCC.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p16 can help separate HPV-associated from HPV-independent components when needed.
entities.reporting_consequence: Report relative percentages of components when feasible.

visualAids.alt: Composite H&E micrograph of penile verrucous carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Mixed SCC teaching reference: verrucous/cuniculatum-like pattern requires search for usual invasive component.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Use when HPV association or subtype cannot be determined confidently.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Biopsy-limited or artifact-limited specimens commonly require conservative classification.
entities.morphology_anchor: Invasive SCC without enough evidence for HPV-associated or HPV-independent subtype.
entities.ancillary_anchor: p16 unavailable, uninterpretable, or discordant with morphology.
entities.reporting_consequence: Use NOS honestly rather than forcing a subtype; still report staging-critical features.

visualAids.alt: Intermediate-power H&E micrograph of penile squamous cell carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Biopsy-limited invasive squamous cell carcinoma, NOS.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Eczematous or erythematous lesion; may be primary cutaneous or secondary to underlying carcinoma.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Map margins and evaluate for invasive or underlying adnexal/urothelial carcinoma.
entities.morphology_anchor: Paget cells within epidermis or mucosa.
entities.ancillary_anchor: CK7, GATA3, urothelial and GI markers used according to differential.
entities.reporting_consequence: Triggers search for invasive disease and appropriate clinical correlation.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of extramammary Paget disease
visualAids.caption: Extramammary Paget disease image showing large pale Paget cells in epidermis.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.clinical_context: Pigmented or amelanotic penile lesion; rare but high-stakes.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Biopsy must preserve depth assessment; excision requires margin evaluation.
entities.morphology_anchor: Atypical melanocytic proliferation with invasive or in situ component.
entities.ancillary_anchor: SOX10, S100, Melan-A, HMB45 support melanocytic lineage.
entities.reporting_consequence: Report melanoma-specific depth and margin parameters; do not force SCC workflow.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of melanoma
visualAids.caption: Melanoma morphology that should stop a squamous carcinoma sign-out pathway.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the penis and scrotum
entities.entity: Mesenchymal, hematolymphoid, and metastatic tumors of the penis
entities.clinical_context: Mass-forming lesion not fitting SCC; may represent sarcoma, lymphoma, or metastasis.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Tissue triage for flow cytometry or molecular testing may matter on biopsy.
entities.morphology_anchor: Non-squamous morphology should stop the SCC algorithm.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Lineage-specific broad panel guided by morphology.
entities.reporting_consequence: Prevents wrong staging template and wrong treatment pathway.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of spindle cell sarcoma
visualAids.caption: Spindle-cell sarcoma morphology in the differential diagnosis of a non-squamous penile mass.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
visualAids.caption: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma morphology in the differential diagnosis of a penile mass.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Adjacent to most postpubertal malignant germ cell tumors.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Look in background testis on orchiectomy sections.
entities.morphology_anchor: Atypical germ cells along basement membrane of seminiferous tubules with impaired spermatogenesis.
entities.ancillary_anchor: OCT4, SALL4, PLAP, and CD117 support diagnosis in the right tubules.
entities.reporting_consequence: Confirms the postpubertal germ cell tumor pathway and frames adjacent invasive tumor biology.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of germ cell neoplasia in situ
visualAids.caption: GCNIS showing atypical germ cells along seminiferous tubule basement membranes.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.family: Specific forms of intratubular germ cell neoplasia
entities.entity: Specific forms of intratubular germ cell neoplasia
entities.clinical_context: Seen adjacent to invasive GCT or in extensively sampled orchiectomy specimens.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Requires background testis sampling; usually not a gross diagnosis.
entities.morphology_anchor: Intratubular growth resembling a specific invasive germ cell component.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Component-directed stains: OCT4/CD117 for seminoma, CD30 for embryonal carcinoma, hCG for trophoblast.
entities.reporting_consequence: Supports lineage/pathway but usually does not supersede invasive tumor reporting.

visualAids.alt: Low-power H&E micrograph of intertubular seminoma
visualAids.caption: Intratubular and intertubular seminoma showing subtle intratubular and interstitial spread.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.family: Non-invasive germ cell and sex cord lesion
entities.clinical_context: Dysgenetic gonad with Y-chromosome material; high propensity for invasive seminoma/germinoma.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Often prophylactic gonadectomy or dysgenetic gonad specimen.
entities.morphology_anchor: Nests of germ cells and immature sex cord cells, often with calcification.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Germ cell and sex cord markers support dual population.
entities.reporting_consequence: Communicates invasive transformation risk and syndromic context.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of gonadoblastoma
visualAids.caption: Gonadoblastoma with mixed germ cell and sex cord elements.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Postpubertal testicular mass, often fourth decade; beta-hCG may be mildly elevated with syncytiotrophoblasts.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Homogeneous tan mass; sample thoroughly to exclude NSGCT component.
entities.morphology_anchor: Uniform clear cells, crisp borders, fibrous septa, lymphocytes.
entities.ancillary_anchor: OCT4/SALL4/CD117 positive, CD30 negative; i(12p)/12p amplification in postpubertal pathway.
entities.reporting_consequence: Do not call pure seminoma with elevated AFP; mixed tumor treatment implications are major.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of seminoma
visualAids.caption: Classic seminoma image showing uniform clear cells and lymphocyte-rich septa.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Aggressive NSGCT component in young adults; often mixed.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Hemorrhagic/necrotic mass; sample heterogeneous areas.
entities.morphology_anchor: High-grade primitive epithelial cells in solid, glandular, or papillary patterns with necrosis.
entities.ancillary_anchor: CD30 and OCT4 positive; CD117 usually negative.
entities.reporting_consequence: Quantify component in mixed GCT and flag aggressive biology.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of embryonal carcinoma
visualAids.caption: Embryonal carcinoma image showing high-grade primitive epithelial morphology.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: NSGCT component; serum AFP elevation is a critical clue.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Sample extensively when AFP is discordant with morphology.
entities.morphology_anchor: Reticular/microcystic, glandular, papillary, solid, hepatoid, or Schiller-Duval patterns.
entities.ancillary_anchor: SALL4, Glypican-3, AFP, and cytokeratin support diagnosis.
entities.reporting_consequence: Pure seminoma is unsafe if AFP is elevated; report component percentage.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of yolk sac tumor of the testis
visualAids.caption: Yolk sac tumor image for AFP-linked NSGCT morphology.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Highly aggressive hemorrhagic tumor, often with markedly elevated hCG.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Hemorrhage/necrosis may dominate; sample viable tumor.
entities.morphology_anchor: Biphasic cytotrophoblast and syncytiotrophoblast without chorionic villi.
entities.ancillary_anchor: hCG highlights syncytiotrophoblast; trophoblastic marker support as needed.
entities.reporting_consequence: Even small components matter because of aggressive hematogenous spread.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of choriocarcinoma
visualAids.caption: Choriocarcinoma with biphasic trophoblastic morphology.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Rare trophoblastic differentiations in GCT context.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Usually discovered during component-level review of mixed GCT.
entities.morphology_anchor: Monophasic or cystic trophoblastic differentiation distinct from classic choriocarcinoma.
entities.ancillary_anchor: hCG, inhibin, Mel-CAM, p63, and Ki-67 patterns may help by subtype.
entities.reporting_consequence: Document as component when clinically meaningful; correlate with serum hCG.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of seminoma with syncytiotrophoblasts
visualAids.caption: Syncytiotrophoblastic differentiation in an hCG-producing trophoblastic lesion.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Postpubertal teratoma behaves as a malignant germ cell tumor even when mature.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Cystic/solid heterogeneous tumor; sample all different gross textures.
entities.morphology_anchor: Somatic tissues from one or more germ layers in postpubertal GCT setting.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Diagnosis is morphology and clinical-pathway based; 12p supports postpubertal GCT pathway.
entities.reporting_consequence: Quantify because teratoma is chemo-resistant and drives surgery after chemotherapy.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of teratoma
visualAids.caption: This teratoma image shows somatic tissue differentiation; in a postpubertal testis tumor, report teratoma percentage because residual teratoma is chemotherapy-resistant and surgically managed.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Primary or metastatic teratoma with frankly malignant somatic component.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Post-chemo RPLND and orchiectomy specimens require sampling of firm/solid residual nodules.
entities.morphology_anchor: Malignant somatic neoplasm arising in teratomatous background; PNET terminology replaced by embryonic neuroectodermal tumor.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Component-directed immunostains/molecular testing; compare with sarcoma/carcinoma differential.
entities.reporting_consequence: Name the somatic malignancy; it can change therapy away from standard GCT regimens.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of mixed germ cell tumor
visualAids.caption: This mixed GCT image shows heterogeneous tumor composition; sample additional grossly distinct areas and report any somatic-type malignancy by lineage and percentage.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours derived from GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Default reality for many postpubertal GCTs.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Sample widely; component percentages depend on thorough sampling.
entities.morphology_anchor: Two or more germ cell tumor components.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Use component-directed stains only after morphology identifies candidate areas.
entities.reporting_consequence: Report each component percentage; teratoma and embryonal/yolk sac elements affect management.

visualAids.alt: Intermediate-power H&E micrograph of mixed germ cell tumor
visualAids.caption: Mixed germ cell tumor image showing more than one component in the same tumor.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours unrelated to GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Usually occurs in older patients, is often indolent, and is unrelated to GCNIS.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Orchiectomy mass; background GCNIS generally absent.
entities.morphology_anchor: Three-cell population with edematous or sheet-like growth.
entities.ancillary_anchor: OCT4 and CD117 are not the classic seminoma profile; molecular/pathway differs from postpubertal GCT.
entities.reporting_consequence: Avoid overtreatment as seminoma/mixed GCT; recognize sarcomatous transformation if present.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of spermatocytic tumor
visualAids.caption: Spermatocytic tumor showing the non-GCNIS-associated morphology typical of this entity.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours unrelated to GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Rare aggressive transformation of otherwise indolent spermatocytic tumor.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Sample firm, fleshy, necrotic, or high-grade appearing areas.
entities.morphology_anchor: Spermatocytic tumor associated with high-grade sarcomatous component.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Lineage stains characterize sarcomatous differentiation; exclude de novo sarcoma.
entities.reporting_consequence: Transforms prognosis and requires explicit diagnosis.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of testicular spermatocytic tumor
visualAids.caption: Spermatocytic tumor; sarcomatous areas require separate sampling when present.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours unrelated to GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Common malignant testicular tumor of young children.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Pediatric orchiectomy or testis-sparing context; background lacks GCNIS.
entities.morphology_anchor: Yolk sac patterns in a prepubertal clinical-pathway tumor.
entities.ancillary_anchor: AFP/Glypican-3/SALL4 support diagnosis; no GCNIS.
entities.reporting_consequence: Age/pathway changes prognosis and management expectations.

visualAids.alt: Intermediate-power H&E micrograph of yolk sac tumor
visualAids.caption: This yolk sac tumor image shows pediatric/prepubertal-type morphology; interpret it with age, AFP, and absence of GCNIS or 12p amplification.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours unrelated to GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Pediatric or prepubertal-type teratoma; generally benign behavior when unrelated to GCNIS.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Cystic lesion; sample to exclude other components.
entities.morphology_anchor: Mature somatic tissues without a GCNIS background.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Absence of GCNIS and 12p amplification supports prepubertal-type pathway.
entities.reporting_consequence: Do not apply postpubertal malignant-teratoma assumptions when pathway supports prepubertal type.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of dermoid cyst
visualAids.caption: Dermoid/prepubertal-type teratoma showing benign teratomatous tissue without GCNIS association.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Germ cell tumours unrelated to GCNIS
entities.clinical_context: Most examples arise with prepubertal-type teratoma; carcinoid terminology is retired.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Sample associated teratomatous elements and exclude metastasis.
entities.morphology_anchor: Well-differentiated neuroendocrine morphology in testicular teratoma pathway.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Synaptophysin/chromogranin/INSM1 support neuroendocrine differentiation; Ki-67 and mitoses may matter.
entities.reporting_consequence: Use neuroendocrine tumor terminology and document association with teratoma/pathway.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of neuroendocrine tumor
visualAids.caption: This neuroendocrine tumor image shows well-differentiated neuroendocrine morphology; use neuroendocrine tumor terminology rather than carcinoid and exclude metastasis.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.clinical_context: Hormone-producing testicular mass; gynecomastia or androgenic symptoms may occur.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Small yellow-brown mass; submit completely if small.
entities.morphology_anchor: Polygonal eosinophilic cells; Reinke crystals support but are not required.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Inhibin, calretinin, SF1 support sex cord-stromal lineage.
entities.reporting_consequence: Assess malignant features including size, necrosis, atypia, vascular invasion, extra-testicular invasion, and mitotic activity per mm2.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of Leydig cell tumor
visualAids.caption: Leydig cell tumor histology reference showing polygonal eosinophilic cells; sex cord-stromal morphology is the teaching target.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
synonyms: large cell calcifying Sertoli cell tumor
entities.clinical_context: Sex cord-stromal tumor; syndromic associations include Peutz-Jeghers and Carney complex for specific forms.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Solid testicular or rete-region mass; sample for invasion and malignant features.
entities.morphology_anchor: Tubules, cords, nests, or solid sex cord differentiation; calcifying and hyalinizing patterns may occur.
entities.ancillary_anchor: SF1, inhibin, calretinin, WT1 support sex cord-stromal lineage.
entities.reporting_consequence: Subtype/syndromic clue and malignant-feature assessment are required.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of Sertoli cell tumor
visualAids.caption: Sertoli cell tumor showing cords and trabeculae of sex cord cells.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.clinical_context: Rare sex cord-stromal tumor; juvenile form is a pediatric consideration.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Cystic/solid mass; submit to assess invasion and exclude mixed tumor.
entities.morphology_anchor: Granulosa-type architecture including microfollicles or juvenile nodular/cystic patterns.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Sex cord markers support; FOXL2 may support adult granulosa in appropriate context.
entities.reporting_consequence: Age-specific pathway and malignant-feature assessment prevent over/under-treatment.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of granulosa cell tumor
visualAids.caption: Granulosa cell tumor with microfollicular and sex cord differentiation.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.clinical_context: Rare tumors often encountered as small indeterminate ultrasound-detected masses.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Submit completely if limited; preserve tissue for broad differential.
entities.morphology_anchor: Sex cord-stromal differentiation not fitting common Leydig/Sertoli/granulosa categories; signet ring and myoid entities are WHO 2022 updates.
entities.ancillary_anchor: SF1/inhibin/calretinin plus pattern-specific markers; beta-catenin may support signet ring stromal tumor.
entities.reporting_consequence: Avoid calling NOS undifferentiated; assess malignant risk features.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of Sertoli-Leydig cell tumor
visualAids.caption: Mixed sex cord-stromal tumor showing uncommon sex cord-stromal patterns.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.clinical_context: Rare epithelial tumors in paratesticular/rete-region differential.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Cystic paratesticular or testicular hilar mass; sample epithelial complexity.
entities.morphology_anchor: Mullerian/ovarian-type epithelial architecture.
entities.ancillary_anchor: PAX8/WT1/CK7 and other markers according to subtype; exclude metastasis.
entities.reporting_consequence: Requires site-of-origin correlation and non-GCT reporting pathway.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of serous cystadenoma
visualAids.caption: Serous cystadenoma showing ovarian-type epithelial tumor morphology.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.category: Tumours of collecting ducts and rete testis
entities.entity: Tumors of the collecting ducts and rete testis
entities.clinical_context: Hilar/rete-centered lesion, usually older adults for malignant tumors.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Map relationship to rete, epididymis, and paratestis.
entities.morphology_anchor: Benign adenomatous or malignant glandular proliferation centered in rete/collecting ducts.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Epithelial markers support; exclude metastatic adenocarcinoma and mesothelioma.
entities.reporting_consequence: Site assignment is the diagnosis; wrong origin changes staging and management.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of adenomatoid tumor
visualAids.caption: Hilar/paratesticular epithelial-pattern lesion in the differential diagnosis of a rete-region tumor.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.clinical_context: Paratesticular/tunica mass or hydrocele-associated lesion.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Tunica vaginalis and paratesticular sampling must document invasion if present.
entities.morphology_anchor: Benign adenomatoid or papillary mesothelial proliferation versus invasive malignant mesothelioma.
entities.ancillary_anchor: Calretinin, WT1, D2-40, keratins support mesothelial lineage; BAP1/MTAP may help malignant differential.
entities.reporting_consequence: WDPMT is favorable if strictly noninvasive; mesothelioma requires explicit invasion and margin assessment.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of adenomatoid tumor
visualAids.caption: Adenomatoid tumor showing benign paratesticular mesothelial morphology.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.clinical_context: Epididymal mass; papillary cystadenoma may suggest von Hippel-Lindau syndrome when bilateral.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Localize to epididymis; sample cystic/papillary areas and invasive front if present.
entities.morphology_anchor: Cystic or papillary epithelial epididymal tumor.
entities.ancillary_anchor: PAX8 and renal-type markers may appear; exclude metastatic RCC.
entities.reporting_consequence: Syndromic clue and primary-vs-metastatic distinction matter.

visualAids.alt: Intermediate-power H&E micrograph of adenomatoid tumor
visualAids.caption: Paratesticular/epididymal mass with benign mesothelial-pattern morphology.

entities.chapter: Tumours of the testis and paratesticular tissue
entities.entity: Hematolymphoid and metastatic tumors of the testis
entities.clinical_context: Older man with testicular mass, often bilateral or systemic; metastases occur but are uncommon.
entities.gross_or_specimen_context: Fresh tissue triage for flow can matter if lymphoma suspected.
entities.morphology_anchor: Diffuse interstitial destructive infiltrate or non-GCT morphology.
entities.ancillary_anchor: CD45 and lineage-specific hematolymphoid markers; metastatic panels guided by history.
entities.reporting_consequence: Avoid orchiectomy-only/GCT treatment assumptions; systemic therapy pathway differs.

visualAids.alt: High-power H&E micrograph of DLBCL of testis
visualAids.caption: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of testis image for the lymphoma diagnosis that must be considered early in older patients.
